comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Scope correction while implementing: dropped the planned __main__.py signpost. The only commit_tree/update_ref in rebar_reconciler/__main__.py is the legacy-lock-file purge (_purge_committed_reconciler_locks), NOT the inbound-event batch commit. Anchoring a batch-write note there would misattribute. The accurate facts: _write_event_file (inbound_translate.py) writes inbound events without a per-event commit; the pass commits them in batch via git_adapter plumbing. Signposts land on the 3 files that actually matter: event_append.py, import_ndjson.py, inbound_translate.py, plus the canonical docs/architecture.md section.

comments.body: Patchset 1 CI failed on the Module-size gate: applier.py was exactly 800 LOC (soft cap) at origin/main and my 4-line signpost pushed it to 804. Fix: dropped the applier.py docstring signpost (reverted to 800). No info lost — docs/architecture.md still names applier._apply_batch explicitly as the outbound-Jira false friend. LLM-Review was already +1 on ps1. Patchset 2 pushed (4 files: architecture.md + 3 docstrings), make lint+typecheck green, module-size safe (all edited files <=800). Awaiting re-vote.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Patchset 2 CI failed on the env-var registry drift gate (test_gen_env_registry): my 9-line docstring in inbound_translate.py shifted an os.environ.get() call-site from line 40->49, and docs/env-vars.md pins call-site line numbers. Root cause of missing it: I ran make lint+typecheck but not the full make test locally. Fix: regenerated docs/env-vars.md (the intended workflow). Patchset 3 pushed (5 files). This time ran FULL make test: 4935 passed, 21 skipped. Awaiting re-vote.

compiled_state.description:
## What
Add agent-facing documentation to stop a recurring confusion: agents scoping "batch writes to the local ticket store" wander into the Jira reconciler (`src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/`) and mistake its internals for the local store write API.

## Why
The reconciler is a CLIENT of the same git-backed store — it shares the low-level lock (`src/rebar/_store/lock.py`, invariant I5) and the canonical-bytes contract — so a grep for "batch"/"commit" lands there. Two specific false friends: `applier._apply_batch` is an OUTBOUND Jira REST sequencer (not a local commit-batcher), and inbound events are written by `inbound_translate._write_event_file` (one file per call under the lock, via `os.replace`, with no `git add`/`commit` — it does NOT go through `stage_and_commit`). The stale epic cold-stall-chalk compounded this with its "WS-B3: batch-apply" wording. This has already misled at least one agent. Fix lands where an agent greps (module docstrings) plus one authoritative section.

## Scope
- Add a "Two writers, one store" section to `docs/architecture.md` (inserted immediately before the "### Python package layout & the engine import boundary" subsection) distinguishing the local store write path (`src/rebar/_store/event_append.py`; citing — not editing — the inline cores in `src/rebar/_commands/txn.py`) from the Jira reconciler (a CLIENT of the store), stating what they share, clearing up the two false friends above, and listing the overloaded vocabulary.
- Add docstring signposts pointing to that section in `src/rebar/_store/event_append.py`, `src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py`, `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_translate.py`, and `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/applier.py` (the `_apply_batch` false-friend).
- No code behavior changes (docs + docstrings only).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `docs/architecture.md` has a "Two writers, one store" section naming the two writers (the local `rebar._store` write path vs. the Jira reconciler as a client of the store).
- [ ] The section states the two writers share ONLY `rebar._store.lock` + the canonical-bytes contract, with no shared write API.
- [ ] The section clears up `applier._apply_batch` as an OUTBOUND Jira REST sequencer, not a local commit-batcher.
- [ ] The section clears up `inbound_translate._write_event_file` as writing one event file per call with no `git add`/`commit` (not via `stage_and_commit`).
- [ ] The section lists the overloaded vocabulary: reconcile / apply / batch / sync.
- [ ] A docstring cross-ref to the section is added in `src/rebar/_store/event_append.py`.
- [ ] A docstring cross-ref to the section is added in `src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py`.
- [ ] A docstring cross-ref to the section is added in `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_translate.py`.
- [ ] A docstring cross-ref to the section is added in `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/applier.py` (at `_apply_batch`, noting it is outbound Jira REST, not a local commit-batcher).
- [ ] `make lint` passes.
- [ ] `make typecheck` passes.

## Notes
- The `__main__.py` signpost from the original plan was intentionally dropped: the only `commit_tree`/`update_ref` there is the legacy-lock-file purge, not an event-file batch commit — anchoring a batch-write note there would misattribute.
- The stale `cold-stall-chalk` WS-B3 "batch-apply" wording is being corrected concurrently by the epic-decomposition work (tracked under that epic), so it is out of scope here.

compiled_state.title: docs: disambiguate local ticket-store write path vs. Jira reconciler ('Two writers, one store')

verify_commands.command: grep -q _apply_batch docs/architecture.md && grep -q _write_event_file docs/architecture.md && grep -q outbound docs/architecture.md
verify_commands.dd_text: Section clears up _apply_batch (outbound Jira REST) and _write_event_file (no commit)

verify_commands.command: grep -q canonical docs/architecture.md && grep -q 'Overloaded vocabulary' docs/architecture.md
verify_commands.dd_text: Section states shared lock+canonical bytes and lists overloaded vocabulary

verify_commands.command: for f in src/rebar/_store/event_append.py src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_translate.py; do grep -q 'Two writers, one store' "$f" || exit 1; done
verify_commands.dd_text: Three module docstrings cross-ref the section
